summary: Task 6 implementation is correct, aligned with spec, and passes every explicit constraint in the review brief. Decision 9 is respected literally — the RuntimeException is thrown inline from AlertDialog.confirmButton.onClick on the main thread, no Handler/Thread/launch indirection, and the dialog is dismissed BEFORE the throw (matching the task hint about composition cleanup and :crash restart flash). Decision 12 is respected — the gesture path is not wrapped in BuildConfig.DEBUG. The in-flight guard is wired correctly: launching flips to true synchronously in Button.onClick, the result callback runs a coroutineScope.launch { try { ... } finally { launching = false } }, and the finally reliably resets the flag on all three outcomes (success branch, onFailure branch, and the uri == null cancel branch which falls through the if). TapCounter is remember-ed with `{ System.nanoTime() }` as a lambda (not ::System.nanoTime bound-reference that the task hint explicitly warns about), default maxGapNanos/threshold are not overridden, and showDialog state lives in the composable, not inside TapCounter — consistent with Variant A from the task. Hilt wiring is clean: @HiltViewModel class AboutViewModel obtained via hiltViewModel() resolves through MainActivity (which remains @AndroidEntryPoint), no @AndroidEntryPoint on the composable, and the new LogExportModule @Binds CommandRunner and DeviceInfoProvider on SingletonComponent without conflicting with the hand-built graph in LogExportManager's secondary constructor used by :crash — DefaultCommandRunner's added @Inject constructor() is still a zero-arg constructor at the JVM level, so `DefaultCommandRunner()` from LogcatReader's no-arg secondary constructor and LogExportManager(Context) both compile and run. All 7 string keys (about_diagnostics_title, log_export_save_button, log_export_success_toast, log_export_error_toast, dev_crash_dialog_title, dev_crash_dialog_confirm, btn_cancel) resolve in values/strings.xml; no hardcoded Russian, no HardcodedText lint risk. No regression in existing AboutScreen behaviour: produceState(about.md load), TopAppBar back button, HorizontalDivider→AboutFooter order are all preserved verbatim. Compose UI is intentionally not covered by unit tests (patterns.md), so the absence of new tests is expected for this task. No critical, high, or medium findings. Two low-severity observations worth mentioning but not worth blocking the task.

findings.description: If saveLogLauncher.launch(...) were ever to throw synchronously (e.g. ActivityNotFoundException on an OEM without DocumentsUI), launching would be set to true in the preceding line and never cleared — the Button stays disabled until the composable is recomposed from scratch. In practice CreateDocument is served by system DocumentsUI which is present on stock Android and on Honor 200, so this branch is not reachable on the target device; and even if hit, leaving+reopening About recomposes and resets the flag.
findings.suggestion: Optional hardening for a later pass: wrap the launcher.launch call in try { ... } catch (e: ActivityNotFoundException) { launching = false; coroutineScope.launch { snackbarHostState.showSnackbar(errorMessage) } }. Not required for Task 6 scope — the acceptance criteria do not mention this path and Honor 200 is unaffected.

findings.description: The SAF result callback launches buildAndWrite inside rememberCoroutineScope(), which is tied to the composition. If the user navigates back from About while buildExport/writeTo is still running, the coroutine is cancelled mid-write and partial bytes may already be in the SAF-chosen file. For ≤300 KB text this is effectively instantaneous in practice, and the user-driven SAF picker already takes focus off the screen, so this edge case is extremely unlikely to be observed.
findings.suggestion: Non-blocking. If ever felt in practice, move buildAndWrite to viewModelScope inside AboutViewModel (viewModelScope survives the ViewModel owner, which is MainActivity here) and expose completion via a SharedFlow<Result<Unit>> collected by a LaunchedEffect in the composable. Not required for Phase 2.5 — buildExport already uses Dispatchers.IO and the write is a single stream.write of ≤~300 KB.
